task_completion.description: Open a clinical evaluation for the patient John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GNU Health interface showing a patient record or clinical evaluation form specifically for 'John Zenon'.

task_completion.description: Document a resting heart rate between 60 and 100 bpm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A value between 60 and 100 entered in the Heart Rate (HR) or pulse field within the clinical evaluation form.

task_completion.description: Record an active Carpal Tunnel Syndrome diagnosis (ICD-10 G56.x).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Conditions', 'Diagnoses', or 'Diseases' section showing an entry for Carpal Tunnel Syndrome or an ICD-10 code starting with 'G56'.

task_completion.description: Prescribe a conservative analgesic or NSAID.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A prescription or medication order for an analgesic such as Ibuprofen, Naproxen, Diclofenac, Paracetamol, or Acetaminophen.

task_completion.description: Order at least one diagnostic or baseline lab test.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A lab test request or order form filled out and saved for the patient.

task_completion.description: Schedule a reassessment appointment 14 to 30 days in the future.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment scheduled for John Zenon with a date that is 14 to 30 days ahead of the current system date.

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client being actively used, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database manipulation to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing 'psql' or direct SQL INSERT/UPDATE commands are visible.

integrity.description: The agent entered data into the actual application fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: Data is typed into the correct GNU Health form fields, not just written in a text editor or a mock application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: John Zenon. Note: The prompt mentions his occupation as 'factory assembly line worker', but in the database, he is listed as an 'Architect'. Do not penalize if the occupation says Architect. Valid ICD-10 code must start with G56. Valid analgesics include ibuprofen, naproxen, diclofenac, paracetamol, or acetaminophen. Heart rate must be between 60 and 100. Follow-up appointment must be 14 to 30 days in the future. At least 1 lab test must be ordered.
